tree.setup: You've been on hold for twelve minutes, listening to a loop of synthesized guzheng music that has become disturbingly familiar. Your internet hasn't worked for two days. Finally, customer service agent Xiao Li picks up — a young man whose voice suggests he has already handled approximately nine hundred complaints today, and yet somehow maintains the warmth of someone who genuinely wants to help.
tree.setup_zh: 你已经等了十二分钟，听着循环播放的合成古筝音乐，熟悉到令人不安。你的网络已经两天不能用了。终于，客服小李接了电话——一个年轻人，声音听起来今天已经处理了大约九百个投诉，但不知怎么还保持着真心想帮忙的温暖。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 您好，这里是网络客服中心。请问您遇到了什么问题？
turns.text_pinyin: nín hǎo, zhèlǐ shì wǎngluò kèfú zhōngxīn. qǐngwèn nín yùdào le shénme wèntí?
turns.text_en: Hello, this is the internet customer service center. What problem are you experiencing?

options.text_zh: 你好，我家的网络两天没有信号了。路由器重启过了，但是还是连不上。
options.text_pinyin: nǐ hǎo, wǒ jiā de wǎngluò liǎng tiān méiyǒu xìnhào le. lùyóuqì chóngqǐ guò le, dànshì háishì lián bú shàng.
options.text_en: Hello, my home internet has had no signal for two days. I've restarted the router but still can't connect.
options.feedback: Duration plus troubleshooting already attempted — Xiao Li skips three steps in his script. You've saved you both five minutes.
Clear but sparse. He'll walk through the standard checklist with you.

options.text_pinyin: nǐmen de wǎngluò tài chà le!
options.feedback: Xiao Li absorbs this with the practiced calm of a man who has a stress ball just off-camera.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我理解您很着急。我先查一下您的账户。请问您的手机号码是多少？
turns.text_pinyin: wǒ lǐjiě nín hěn zháojí. wǒ xiān chá yíxià nín de zhànghù. qǐngwèn nín de shǒujī hàomǎ shì duōshǎo?
turns.text_en: I understand you're frustrated. Let me check your account first. What's your phone number?

options.text_zh: 我的手机号是138-1234-5678。账户名是我的名字。
options.text_pinyin: wǒ de shǒujī hào shì yāo sān bā, yāo èr sān sì, wǔ liù qī bā. zhànghù míng shì wǒ de míngzi.
options.text_en: My number is 138-1234-5678. The account is under my name.
options.feedback: Using 幺 (yāo) for 1 on the phone — the correct way to avoid confusion with 七 (qī). Xiao Li types it in immediately.

options.text_pinyin: yāo sān bā, yāo èr sān sì, wǔ liù qī bā.
options.feedback: Efficient. He pulls up your account.

options.text_zh: 等一下，我找找看我的号码……
options.text_pinyin: děng yíxià, wǒ zhǎozhǎo kàn wǒ de hàomǎ……
options.text_en: Wait, let me look for my number...
options.feedback: Xiao Li waits. He has waited for longer. He has waited for people who never found their number.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 找到了。我看到您那边确实有一个网络故障。我们的技术人员明天下午可以去您家检查。您明天在家吗？
turns.text_pinyin: zhǎo dào le. wǒ kàn dào nín nàbiān quèshí yǒu yí gè wǎngluò gùzhàng. wǒmen de jìshù rényuán míngtiān xiàwǔ kěyǐ qù nín jiā jiǎnchá. nín míngtiān zài jiā ma?
turns.text_en: Found it. I can see there is indeed a network issue on your end. Our technician can come check tomorrow afternoon. Will you be home?

options.text_zh: 明天下午可以。大概几点？我需要请半天假。
options.text_pinyin: míngtiān xiàwǔ kěyǐ. dàgài jǐ diǎn? wǒ xūyào qǐng bàn tiān jiǎ.
options.text_en: Tomorrow afternoon works. What time roughly? I'll need to take a half day off.
options.feedback: Asking for a time window — because「下午」is the vaguest appointment in human history, and Xiao Li knows it.

options.text_pinyin: hǎo de, wǒ zài jiā děng.
options.feedback: Agreeable. He'll give you a range.

options.text_pinyin: néng bu néng jīntiān jiù lái?
options.feedback: Xiao Li checks. Today's slots are full — tomorrow is the best he can do.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 大概两点到四点之间。技术人员会提前打电话给您。还有什么问题吗？
turns.text_pinyin: dàgài liǎng diǎn dào sì diǎn zhī jiān. jìshù rényuán huì tíqián dǎ diànhuà gěi nín. hái yǒu shénme wèntí ma?
turns.text_en: Between 2 and 4 PM roughly. The technician will call you in advance. Any other questions?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Thank him and close the call.

options.text_zh: 没有了。谢谢你，小李。你的态度很好，帮了我大忙。
options.text_pinyin: méiyǒu le. xièxie nǐ, Xiǎo Lǐ. nǐ de tàidu hěn hǎo, bāng le wǒ dà máng.
options.text_en: No more questions. Thank you, Xiao Li. Your attitude is great — you've been a big help.
options.feedback: Complimenting a service agent's attitude — Xiao Li's stress ball goes untouched for the first time today.
Clean exit. The guzheng music resumes for the next caller.
Xiao Li hopes so too. He genuinely does.

tree.cultural_note: When reading phone numbers aloud in Chinese, 1 is often pronounced 幺 (yāo) instead of 一 (yī) to avoid confusion. Chinese customer service agents are addressed as 客服 (kèfú) and typically follow strict scripts, but personal warmth still shines through.
